section:
Class List Here are the classes, structs, unions and interfaces with brief descriptions:Mochi::Graphics::AbstractGUIMochi::Graphics::AbstractTextureFactory
This class must be inherited to generate textures of a compatible type with its own renderer Mochi::Input::ActionMochi::Input::ActionManagerMochi::ActionManagerSwappedEventMochi::Graphics::AnimatedSpriteMochi::Graphics::AnimationsDataMochi::ApplicationQuitEventMochi::Graphics::AsepriteAnimationFactoryMochi::AudioManagerSwappedEventMochi::AudioOperationErrorMochi::Graphics::CameraMochi::CameraSwappedEventMochi::Time::ChronoMochi::Physics::CircleMochi::ColorMochi::DebugLayerMochi::Graphics::DummyAnimationFactoryMochi::Audio::DummyAudioManagerMochi::Event::DummyEventDispatcherMochi::Input::DummyGamepadProviderMochi::Graphics::DummyGizmosMochi::Graphics::DummyGUIMochi::Input::DummyKeyboardProviderMochi::Input::DummyMouseProviderMochi::Graphics::DummyRendererMochi::Graphics::DummyTextureMochi::Graphics::DummyTextureFactoryMochi::Engine
Main class of the MochiEngine. It owns and coordinates all subsystems and layers, and drives the application lifecycle Mochi::EngineErrorMochi::Event::EventBusMochi::FS::FileEntryMochi::Audio::FMODMarkerEventMochi::Audio::FMODWrapperMochi::Graphics::FrameDataMochi::Debug::FrameProfilerMochi::Graphics::FrameTagMochi::GamepadAddedEventMochi::Input::GamepadDataMochi::GamepadRemovedEventMochi::Graphics::GUIButtonOptionsMochi::Graphics::GUIOptionsMochi::Graphics::GUIResultMochi::Graphics::GUIResultButtonMochi::Graphics::GUITextOptionsMochi::Input::IActionManagerMochi::Graphics::IAnimationFactoryMochi::Audio::IAudioManagerMochi::FS::IFileLoader
Interface to get files that are part of the game data. Note that the interface does not have any save or write function. This is not meant to create persistent file systems, but to create virtual file systems from which to read Mochi::Input::IGamepadProviderMochi::Debug::IGizmosMochi::Input::IKeyboardProviderMochi::Input::IMouseProviderMochi::Input::InputManagerMochi::Input::IPerformableActionMochi::Graphics::IRenderer
This interface provides means to show graphics in the game and create the subsystems needed to create textures for the same renderer and GUI using the same renderer. The interface provides separate methods for rendering to meet the needs of different kind of renderers (initialize, load to gpu, present, for example) Mochi::Event::ISystemEventDispatcherMochi::Graphics::ITexture
A texture interface to be implemented by renderer specific textures Mochi::Layer
The layer class offers a simple way to organize domains of the application while having access to most of the Engine subsystems. Note that a layer is meant to be inherited from and can't be instantated as is. A layer will Update, Render and show the GUI as a small Engine class. So the layer is just a convenient responsability separator. Ownership: Mochi::Physics::LineMochi::MalformedInputActionMochi::Graphics::OneshotAnimationMochi::FS::PackCatalog
The pack catalog is a class that creates and owns file loaders and acts as a combined catalog for all of them at once. This means that opening pack A and pack B allows you to use GetFile with files that are in any or both of them. As it searches in reverse order of pack opening, files of the last opened packs are prioritized when using GetFile and HasFile, which allows you to "override" assets of a previous pack, allowing a simple but effective way of modding. Each file system inside the catalog starts at the root, so it is needed the same folder structure in two file systems to be able to override. Example: Pack A Mochi::FS::PackFile
PackFile is a class with two purposes. First, it implements IFileLoader, allowing to be used as a simple file system. Second, it has the Pack and Unpack methods, used to create .pak files, used by this file system. Creating .pak files has several benefits. The first one is obfuscation, making it more difficult to end users to get access to your game assets. The second one is being loaded as a stream, as it allows to only load what is needed at the moment. It also is easily extended to, for example, zip all or only specific files, reducing final build size. Ownership: Mochi::FS::PackHeaderMochi::Input::PerformableActionGamepadMochi::Input::PerformableActionGamepadAxisMochi::Input::PerformableActionKeyboardMochi::Input::PerformableActionMouseMochi::Input::PerformableActionMouseAxisMochi::Physics::PointMochi::Physics::RectangleMochi::RectfMochi::Graphics::RenderCommandMochi::RendererSwappedEventMochi::ResourceNotFoundErrorMochi::Scripting::ScriptingLayerMochi::Scripting::ScriptingManagerMochi::Input::SDLGamepadProviderMochi::Debug::SDLGizmosMochi::Graphics::SDLGUIMochi::Input::SDLKeyboardProviderMochi::Input::SDLMouseProviderMochi::Graphics::SDLRendererMochi::Event::SDLSystemEventDispatcherMochi::Graphics::SDLTextureMochi::Graphics::SDLTextureFactoryMochi::Physics::ShapeMochi::Graphics::SpriteBaseMochi::Graphics::SpritesheetMochi::FS::SystemFileLoader
This class creates a very simple virtual file system from a directory. This allows read access to all files in that directory referencing them from their path as a unique identifier. As this doesn't give the benefits of asset obfuscation, it is only used on debug mode, and can't be configured at the moment Mochi::SystemInitializationErrorMochi::Graphics::TiledRenderOptionsMochi::Time::TimerMochi::Time::TimeSystemMochi::TransformMochi::Vector2fMochi::Input::VirtualActionManager
